outcome_text: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI-related legal and regulatory requirements, including the ability to identify applicable regulations, assess organizational compliance status, manage regulatory obligations, and maintain documentation of legal requirements across the AI lifecycle.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI-related legal and regulatory requirements, including the ability to identify applicable regulations, assess organizational compliance status, manage regulatory obligations, and maintain documentation of legal requirements across the AI lifecycle.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-1.1 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.716, cosine: 0.824). Secondary: AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.806), SP-K-006 (SP, cosine: 0.757).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.716). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations, regulatory, requirements]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, across, applicable, assess, identify, legal] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, high] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.913 (P=0.912, R=0.914). Balanced P/R — the 31-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.824 > STS 0.716 (gap +0.108). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 27.8%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations, regulatory, requirements]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, across, applicable, assess, identify, legal]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, high].
